title: Merck — Transplant — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in Merck Transplant — Q4 2025

Generated: 2026-04-26 (retrospective) Quarter covered: October - December 2025 Data sources: Merck Q4 2025 earnings (February 2026), ZoomRx Message Recall Study Wave 1 report, competitive intelligence Reading time: ~10 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. Prevymis FY 2025 revenue: $978M (+25% YoY) — approaching the $1B milestone. Q4 alone was $275M (+28%), the strongest quarter yet. Kidney transplant adoption is the primary growth driver.
  2. Message Recall Study report delivered with a critical finding: the most effective KT message ("no CMV cases") is the least delivered. This is the single biggest actionable insight — KT7 drives high-impact interactions but has the lowest recall rate. Under-delivery of this message is costing Merck prescribing conversions.
  3. Prevymis safety messaging (leukopenia 26% vs. 64%) is the anchor message, but efficacy messaging closes the deal. The data shows a clear two-step promotional path: safety opens the door, efficacy (KT7) converts. Current rep behavior over-indexes on the opener and under-delivers the closer.
  4. Access/cost remains the stated barrier, but framing matters. HCPs want practical access support (prior auth help), not promotional savings messages ($15 co-pay card). The current access messaging is not landing despite strong HCP demand for access information.
  5. Record US kidney transplant volume in 2024: 27,759 — the addressable market is growing. Combined with rising KT volumes and Prevymis's improving promotional effectiveness, the trajectory toward $1B+ is on track.

2. Data Events That Matter

ZoomRx Message Recall Study — The Messaging Gap Discovery

  • What happened: The Wave 1 Message Recall Study report was delivered, covering 102 KT HCPs and 93 HSCT HCPs surveyed May-August 2025.
  • Key findings:
    • KT messaging gap: KT7 ("No CMV cases with Prevymis vs. 5 cases with valganciclovir through Week 28") is the most effective message (68% effectiveness) and significantly drives high-impact interactions — but has the lowest recall rate. This is the clearest "high effectiveness, low delivery" opportunity in the data
    • HSCT messaging gap: HSCT4 (survival benefit through Week 48) and HSCT5 (200-day efficacy) are highly effective but trail industry averages in recall — same pattern as KT
    • Rep performance strong: 75% of interactions rated high quality in both KT and HSCT. >50% prescribing intent increase (vs. 49% industry average)
    • SOV leadership confirmed: Prevymis leads Livtencity and CytoGam in both personal and non-personal promotion share
    • Access paradox: Low recall of access messages despite high HCP demand for access/reimbursement information
  • What Vinoth should say: "The Wave 1 data gives you a clear promotional optimization roadmap. Your reps are performing well — 75% high-quality calls — but they're leading with safety when they should be closing with efficacy. The 'no CMV cases' message is your most powerful tool and it's barely being used. Fix this one thing and you move the prescribing needle."

Merck FY 2025 Earnings — Prevymis Approaching $1B

  • What happened: Merck reported FY 2025 Prevymis revenue of $978M (+25% YoY, +23% ex-FX). Q4 2025 was $275M (+28%).
  • What it means: Prevymis will likely cross $1B in 2026. The growth rate is accelerating (H1 was +20.4%, Q4 was +28%), suggesting the KT ramp is steepening, not plateauing. Merck's hospital acute care segment (Prevymis, Bridion, Zerbaxa) combined for ~$861M in Q4 alone — Prevymis is the growth engine within this portfolio.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Prevymis at nearly $1B is no longer a niche franchise — it's a meaningful contributor to Merck's non-Keytruda revenue. The 28% Q4 growth rate signals the KT adoption curve is still early. This franchise needs the same quality of commercial research support as Merck's blockbuster brands."

Record Kidney Transplant Volume — Growing Addressable Market

  • What happened: OPTN data confirmed 27,759 US kidney transplants in 2024 — a record. Living donor transplants (6,418) were the highest since 2019.
  • What it means: The denominator is growing. More transplants = more D+/R- patients = larger addressable market for Prevymis. If the ~20-25% D+/R- prevalence holds, that's ~5,500-6,900 eligible patients annually — up from historical figures.

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Q3 2025 (est.) Q4 2025 (actual) Commentary
Prevymis Revenue ~$240M $275M +28% YoY; best quarter ever
FY 2025 Total — $978M +25% YoY; approaching $1B
KT SOV Leading Leading Prevymis leads Livtencity and CytoGam
KT High-Impact Interactions — ~50% Half of all KT details drive prescribing intent change

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue Key Signal
Takeda Livtencity (maribavir) ~$35M Slow growth; remains limited to treatment-failure setting
Prevymis $275M +28% YoY; best quarter ever; approaching $1B annual pace

Takeda — Livtencity (~$35M): Livtencity grew modestly to ~$35M in Q4, reflecting slow adoption in the CMV treatment-failure niche. Takeda's earnings commentary did not signal any expansion into prophylaxis — Livtencity remains positioned exclusively for patients where existing CMV antivirals (including Prevymis) have failed. The $35M quarterly run rate ($140M annualized) is stable but not competitive with Prevymis's $978M FY2025. Livtencity actually validates the CMV treatment paradigm: its existence confirms that CMV management is a recognized, reimbursed clinical priority, which benefits the market leader in prophylaxis. Prevymis's competitive pressure comes from valganciclovir (generic, ~40x cheaper) at the formulary/P&T committee level, not from branded competitors.

What Merck management said on the Q4 2025 / FY2025 call:

  1. "PREVYMIS is an underappreciated growth asset." This was the most explicit management positioning of Prevymis to date — a signal that the commercial team is lobbying for more airtime. The $978M FY2025 figure approaching $1B gives management a milestone narrative.
  2. The kidney transplant indication was highlighted as a growth driver. Management attributed the 28% Q4 growth to "continued strong uptake in KT" and positioned the franchise as part of Merck's hospital acute care portfolio alongside Bridion and Zerbaxa.
  3. No Prevymis-specific FY2026 guidance was provided. Prevymis revenue is reported within the hospital acute care segment, not broken out individually — limiting visibility for external analysts.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. No analyst questions were asked about Prevymis or transplant on the Q4/FY2025 call. Despite approaching $1B, the product remains outside the scope of analyst attention, which is concentrated on Keytruda LOE, QLEX, GARDASIL China, and FY2026 guidance.
  2. The "underappreciated growth asset" framing from management may be a prelude to more detailed Prevymis disclosure in future quarters — particularly once the $1B milestone is crossed, which creates a natural news cycle.

5. ZoomRx Engagement Health

Study Status Next Milestone
Message Recall Study Wave 1 Report delivered Merck review + decisions on Wave 2
KT ATU Wave 2 Complete Wave 3 timing TBD — recommend H2 2026
Institutional formulary study Not proposed Recommended new research vector

6. Thesis Updates

Thesis Confidence Change Rationale
KT takes Prevymis past $1.5B by 2027 Unchanged (Medium-High) $978M in 2025 at +25% growth supports trajectory. Formulary access remains unvalidated risk
Most effective message is least delivered Strengthened (High) Wave 1 data conclusively confirms the KT7 gap
R+ patients as growth ceiling Unchanged (Medium) No new evidence. Monitor pipeline disclosures
Nephrologists as under-served lever Unchanged (Medium) Data is directional. Needs larger sample confirmation

7. What's Coming Next Quarter

  • Merck Q1 2026 earnings (April 2026) — will Prevymis cross $250M/quarter consistently? Watch for any guidance on 2026 Prevymis-specific outlook
  • Wave 2 commissioning decision — if Merck acts on the messaging gap insight, a Wave 2 study becomes the natural follow-up to measure improvement
  • International KT traction — first full year of EU KT commercial data available
  • Competitive watch: Livtencity expansion plans; any new CMV prophylaxis entrants
